spec_match.rationale: The page now exercises border-shape parser support, one-shape stroke mode, two-shape fill mode, CSSOM round-tripping, malformed-value rejection, border-radius interaction, layout invariance, and clip-path fallback behavior from the CSS Borders 4 border-shape section.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the shape select, border-width slider, fallback checkbox, radius checkbox, and malformed-value button; each changed the live preview and readouts in-page.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still matches the feature-index promise of CSS.supports probes, live DOM injection, and a clip-path fallback, with the added interactive compatibility lab making those claims observable.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces unsupported/fallback behavior, malformed declaration rejection through CSS.supports and CSSOM, border-radius ignored while border-shape computes, and the fact that border-shape changes paint rather than layout geometry.

references.rationale: The concept page now links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5459864205393920 and the CSS Borders 4 border-shape spec section.

design_system.rationale: The new UI uses shared CSS variables, sets color when overriding button backgrounds, avoids raw hex color values, and remains usable in a 390px-wide DevTools snapshot.

summary: Pre-fix DevTools MCP confirmed this page had zero controls, no ChromeStatus link, and raw hex color text in the fallback snippet. Post-fix testing in Chrome 148 at http://localhost:3000/v147/css-border-shape/compatibility-lab/ exercised the shape select, border-width slider, fallback toggle, radius toggle, and malformed-value button; the page reported CSSOM round-tripping for path(), fallback clip-path output, layout staying 220x120, and malformed border-shape rejection. Console and network were clean. The feature conformance route at /v147/css-border-shape/conformance/ reported 6 PASS, 0 FAIL.
